page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated mathematical and matrix operations. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance across several compute and memory metrics. In memory bandwidth testing, the instance achieves a strong cached read-only small block speed of 3768.18 GB/sec, though uncached large block read and write speeds fall into the average tier at 176.07 GB/sec and 69.43 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform in the top tier, with gzip compression reaching 36.88 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 15.37 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong at 322.59 GB/sec. The Passmark CPU score of 68640.57 and database operations score of 22850.95 both rank in the strong tier. For artificial intelligence workloads, the CPU's AMX instructions enable top-tier prompt processing speeds of 3667.57 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models and strong prompt processing of 258.1 tokens/sec for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48s_v7 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require substantial multi-core processing without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring users to provision remote Premium Storage, which shifts storage costs to an external volume model. The server is suited for workloads such as database operations, static web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and CPU-based machine learning or LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 24 physical cores and 192.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. The server lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on Premium Storage capabilities. Benchmarks show strong performance in multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference, alongside top-tier single-threaded compression speeds. It provides a balanced resource profile for enterprise workloads, making it suitable for database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong database and multi-threaded performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and AMX support, optimized for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated mathematical operations
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage configuration
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
